iterations.title: Distil MCP's agent-interface principles and audit the lume CLI against them
Resolve the forks and plan the agent-friendly-CLI slices
E1: --json flag + structured errors + read-verb output
E2: structured --json results for the mutating verbs
E4: per-verb input schema + grammar docs
E5: closeout - retro, done-when, lume close

plan.sketch: E1: --json plumbing + structured errors (P6). Add a global --json flag; a result emitter and an error emitter; under --json, errors print {error:{code,message}} on stderr with stable codes (usage/gate/not_found/schema); exit codes unchanged. Apply structured results to the read/discovery verbs (status, get, schema, entities, snapshot). Human output unchanged without --json. DONE = --json errors+read-verb output are JSON; default output byte-identical; tests.

E2: structured results for the MUTATING verbs under --json (new, open, approve/start/handback/accept/reject/redo, close, reopen, decide, plan add/link, migrate). Each emits one JSON result object (e.g. {verb, workstream, iteration, phase}) under --json; human text unchanged otherwise. DONE = every verb emits structured JSON under --json; default unchanged; tests.

E3: verb-discovery surface (P1) - 'lume verbs' lists the verb catalog (name, one-line summary, arg outline), human lines by default and JSON under --json; the tools/list analogue, joining entities/schema/get. DONE = 'lume verbs' and 'lume verbs --json' enumerate all verbs; test covers both.

E4 (optional): per-verb input schema (principle P2) + documented grammar (principle P4) - represent each verb's args as fetchable data (e.g. 'lume verbs --json' carries an input schema per verb) and document the grammar; no breaking changes. DONE = each verb advertises its args as data; suite green.
E5: closeout - retro (did the agent-friendly surface buy back its cost?), assess done-when clause by clause, lume close.
